Just eat it without doubt, and you will achieve spiritual transformation in seven days,' describing a supernatural encounter.
  • 洞食毕老人曰吾今先行汝后来也言讫不见后日洞忽觉身轻齿发尽换谓弟子曰上清见召不乆当往顾念汝 - Means 'After eating, the old man said, 'I will go first, and you will follow.' After he spoke, he disappeared. Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 勘之奴悉陈状旭都未知其夜女至: This sentence is elliptical and requires parsing to understand the sequence of events: the slave was questioned, revealed the situation, and Xu was unaware that the woman had come at night.
  • 奴泄吾事当逝矣旭方知失奴而悲不自胜: This clause is also elliptical: the slave revealed Xu's affairs, so he should depart, and only then did Xu realize he had lost the slave, causing him great sorrow.
  • 甚知君心然事亦不合长与君往来: This clause is a conditional statement: the woman understands Xu's heart, but the situation does not allow for continued interaction.
  • 运数然耳自此诀别: This clause is a conclusion: it is the course of fate, and from now on, they part ways.
  • 努力修持当速相见也: This clause is a command and a promise: strive to cultivate oneself, and we shall meet again soon.
  • 其大要以心死可以身生保精可以致神: This clause is a paradoxical statement: the main point is that by letting the mind die, one can live the body, and by preserving essence, one can attain spirit.
  • 遂留仙枢龙席隐诀五篇内多隐语亦指验于旭旭洞晓之: This clause is a description of the texts: he left behind five volumes of the 'Hidden Secret of the Dragon Seat and Celestial Pivot,' which contain cryptic phrases and point to Xu for verification, and Xu thoroughly understood them.
  • 将旦而去悲哽执手: This clause is a description of the farewell: he left just before dawn, choked with grief, and held hands.
  • 悲自何来在心所牵耳: This clause is a question and answer: where does this grief come from? It comes from what the heart is attached to.
  • 身为心牵鬼道至矣言讫竦身而上: This clause is a conclusion and an action: when the body is led by the heart, one reaches the path of ghosts, and after speaking, he ascended with a sudden leap.
  • 忽不见室中帏帏器具悉无矣: This clause is a description of a supernatural event: suddenly, everything in the room disappeared.
  • 恍然自失其后寤寐彷佛犹尙往来: This clause is a description of a state of mind: he was suddenly lost in thought, and afterward, in dreams and waking moments, he still seemed to encounter her.
  • 旭大歴初犹在淮泗或有人于益州见之: This clause is a statement of location and observation: Xu was still in Huai and Si during the early Dali period, and some people in Yizhou saw him.
  • 短小美容范多在市肆商货故时人莫能辨也: This clause is a description of Xu's appearance and activities: he was short and had a beautiful appearance, often appeared in the market selling goods, so people at the time could not distinguish him.
  • 仙枢五篇篇后有旭纪事词甚详悉: This clause is a description of the texts: the five volumes of the 'Celestial Pivot' have detailed accounts of Xu's experiences after each volume.

  • 大歴初 - The early Dali period, a time during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 淮泗 - The Huai and Si rivers, regions in eastern China.
  • 益州 - Yizhou, an ancient name for Chengdu, in present-day Sichuan Province.
  • 仙枢龙席隐诀五篇 - The 'Hidden Secret of the Dragon Seat and Celestial Pivot,' a set of esoteric texts.
  • 运数 - The course of fate or destiny.
  • 鬼道 - The path of ghosts or spirits.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 頭陀但笑耳 - The monk (头陀) merely smiled, where '头陀' refers to a type of Buddhist monk known for ascetic practices. '但笑耳' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'merely smiled' or 'just laughed', often used to convey a sense of indifference or nonchalance.
  • 自然拜施钱二百竟亦不受 - Naturally, he received a donation of two hundred coins, but he did not accept it. '自然' can mean 'naturally' or 'of course', emphasizing the spontaneous nature of the donation. '竟亦不受' means 'but he did not accept it'.
  • 须臾出门不知所在 - After a short while, he left, and his whereabouts were unknown. '须臾' means 'a short while' or 'momentarily'.
  • 有两小白角以头枕房门吐气满室 - With two small white horns, with his head resting against the door of the room, he exhaled, filling the room with mist. '以头枕房门' means 'with his head resting against the door of the room'.
  • 及雾散蛇亦不见 - When the mist cleared, the snake was no longer visible. '及雾散' means 'when the mist cleared'.
  • 自然所居室唯容一牀四边才通人行 - The room where Natural lived could only accommodate one bed, with space just enough for a person to walk around. '唯容一牀' means 'only fits one bed', and '四边才通人行' means 'space just enough for a person to walk around'.
  • 白蛇去后常有十余小蛇或大如臂或大如股 - After the white snake left, there were often more than ten smaller snakes, some as large as an arm, others as large as a thigh. '或大如臂或大如股' means 'some as large as an arm, others as large as a thigh'.
  • 旦夕在牀左右或黑或白或吐气或有声各各盘结不相毒螫 - Day and night, they were around the bed, some black, some white, some exhaling, some making sounds, each coiling up without harming each other. '各各盘结' means 'each coiling up', and '不相毒螫' means 'not harming each other'.
  • 又有两虎出入必从人至则隐伏不见 - There were also two tigers that came and went, always appearing when people arrived and hiding when they left. '必从人至' means 'always appearing when people arrive'.
  • 家犬吠虎 - The family dog barked at the tigers. '吠虎' means 'barked at the tigers'.
  • 自居郭中犬留方出 - When Natural lived in the city, the dog stayed behind when he left. '居郭中' means 'lived in the city', and '犬留方出' means 'the dog stayed behind when he left'.
  • 上升之后犬不知所在 - After Natural ascended, the dog's whereabouts were unknown. '上升之后' means 'after ascending'.
  • 或辄诣其中必有变异 - If anyone entered, there would be some strange occurrence. '辄' means 'always' or 'every time', and '变异' means 'strange occurrence' or 'change'.
  • 深山穷谷无所畏怖 - In deep mountains and remote valleys, he was fearless. '无所畏怖' means 'fearless' or 'without fear'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 头陀 - A type of Buddhist monk known for ascetic practices, often associated with self-discipline and simplicity.
  • 后防日 - The 'Festival of the Tenth Month' (后土节), a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the earth god.
  • 自然 - A name that can be interpreted as 'natural' or 'self-natural', suggesting a person who lives in harmony with nature.
  • 蛇 - Snakes are often symbolic in Chinese culture, representing both danger and transformation. In this context, the snake's presence and behavior suggest a supernatural or mystical element.
  • 虎 - Tigers are powerful and revered animals in Chinese culture, often associated with strength and protection.
  • 家犬 - The family dog, which in this context, serves as a protector and a witness to the supernatural events.
  • 上升 - Often used in the context of spiritual or divine ascension, suggesting a transformation or elevation to a higher state.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '冰虎在前后异常腥臭兼言常有天使八人侍侧二童子青衣戴冠八使衣黄又二天神衞其门屏如今壁画诸神手执鎗钜每行止则诸使及神驱斥侍衞' describes a complex scene involving a mythical creature, celestial beings, and divine guardians. It must be read as a single, detailed description.
  • The long sentence '言已将授东极真人之任贞元十年三月三日移入金泉道塲其日云物明媚异于常景自然云此日天真羣仙皆会金泉林中长有鹿未尝避人士女虽众亦驯扰' describes a specific event and its miraculous context, including the gathering of deities and a harmonious environment.
  • The sentence '明日上仙送白鞍一具缕以寳钿以此遗之其地可安居也' describes a divine gift and its significance, indicating a blessing or assurance.
  • The sentences '五月八日金母元君命卢使降之从午至亥六月二十日闻使从午至戍七月一日崔张二使从寅至午' describe specific events and divine visitations, indicating a series of divine interventions or commands.
  • The sentence '多説神仙官府之事言上界好奕棋多音乐语笑率论至道妙之' describes the activities and discussions of divine beings, indicating a description of celestial life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天上自有神非鬼神之神 - Reflects the belief in higher divine beings in Chinese cosmology, distinguishing them from lower spirits.

  • 若得道后悉皆戴冠 - Reflects the Confucian and Taoist traditions where those who have achieved spiritual enlightenment often wear hats as a symbol of their status.
  • 凡斋食切忌尝之 - Reflects the importance of purity and discipline in religious rituals, particularly in Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 尤宜洁净器皿亦尔 - Emphasizes the importance of cleanliness in religious practices, which is a common theme in many Chinese religious traditions.
  • 上天诸神每斋即降而视之 - Reflects the belief that the gods actively participate in and witness religious rituals, a common theme in Chinese religious thought.
  • 深恶不精洁 - Reflects the strong emphasis on purity and cleanliness in Chinese religious practices.
  • 不唯无福亦当获罪 - Reflects the belief in karma and the consequences of one's actions in Chinese religious thought.

  • 其婢即瘥如故 - '其婢' means 'the maid,' '即' means 'immediately,' '瘥' means 'to recover,' and '如故' means 'as before.'
